query: What is the minimum passport validity required to enter Indonesia?
retrieved_texts:
[ORIGINAL Article 1]
Article 1
In this Law what is meant by:
1. Immigration is a matter of the movement of people entering or leaving the territory of Indonesia and its supervision in order to maintain the upholding of state sovereignty.
2. The territory of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as the Territory of Indonesia, is the entire territory of Indonesia as well as certain zones determined by law.
3. The Immigration function is part of state government affairs in providing immigration services, law enforcement, state security, and facilitating community welfare development.
4. Minister is the minister who carries out government affairs in the field of law and human rights.
5. Director General is the Director General of Immigration.
6. The Directorate General of Immigration is the implementing element of the duties and functions of the Ministry of Law and Human Rights in the field of Immigration.
7. An Immigration Officer is an employee who has undergone special Immigration education and has Immigration technical expertise and has the authority to carry out duties and responsibilities under this Law.
8. Immigration Civil Servant Investigators, hereinafter referred to as Immigration PPNS, are Immigration Officials who are authorized by law to carry out investigations into Immigration crimes.
9. Foreigners are people who are not Indonesian citizens.
10. Immigration Management Information System is an information and communication technology system used to collect, process and present information to support operations, management and decision making in carrying out Immigration Functions.
11. The Immigration Office is a technical implementing unit that carries out Immigration Functions in regency, city or sub-district areas.
12. Immigration Checkpoints are checkpoints at seaports, airports, border crossing posts, or other places as points of entry and exit from Indonesian Territory.
13. Travel Documents are official documents issued by authorized officials from a country, the United Nations, or other international organizations for travel between countries which contain the identity of the holder.
14. Immigration Documents are Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and Stay Permits issued by Immigration Officials or foreign service officials.
15. Travel Documents of the Republic of Indonesia are the Passport of the Republic of Indonesia and the Travel Document is the Passport of the Republic of Indonesia.
16. Passport of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as Passport, is a document issued by the Government of the Republic of Indonesia to Indonesian citizens to travel between countries which is valid for a certain period of time.
17. Travel Document as a Passport of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as a Travel Letter as a Passport, is a passport replacement document which is issued in certain circumstances and is valid for a certain period of time.
18. Republic of Indonesia Visa, hereinafter referred to as Visa, is a written statement given by an authorized official at the Representative Office of the Republic of Indonesia or at another place determined by the Government of the Republic of Indonesia which contains approval for Foreigners to travel to Indonesian Territory and is the basis for granting a Stay Permit.
19. Entry Mark is a certain mark in the form of a stamp affixed to the Travel Documents of Indonesian citizens and Foreigners, both manual and electronic, which are given by Immigration Officials as a sign that the person concerned has entered Indonesian Territory.
20. Exit Mark is a certain mark in the form of a stamp affixed to the Travel Documents of Indonesian citizens and Foreigners, both manual and electronic, which are given by Immigration Officials as a sign that the person concerned has left Indonesian Territory.
21. A Stay Permit is a permit given to a Foreigner by an Immigration Officer or foreign service official to be in Indonesian Territory.
22. Integration Statement is a statement by a Foreigner to the Government of the Republic of Indonesia as one of the conditions for obtaining a Permanent Stay Permit.
23. Permanent Residence Permit is a permit granted to certain Foreigners to reside and settle in the Territory of Indonesia as residents of Indonesia.
24. A Re-Entry Permit is a written permit given by an Immigration Officer to a Foreigner holding a Limited Stay Permit and Permanent Stay Permit to re-enter Indonesian Territory.
25. A corporation is an organized collection of people and/or assets, whether a legal entity or non-legal entity.
26. Guarantor is a person or corporation that is responsible for the presence and activities of foreigners while they are in Indonesian territory.
27. Transportation means are ships, airplanes, or other means of transportation that are commonly used, both to transport people and goods.
28. Prevention is a temporary prohibition on people leaving Indonesian territory based on immigration reasons or other reasons determined by law.
29. Deterrence is a prohibition on foreigners from entering Indonesian territory based on immigration reasons.
30. Immigration Intelligence is the activity of Immigration investigation and Immigration security in the framework of the process of presenting information through analysis in order to determine estimates of the Immigration situation being faced or to be faced.
31. Immigration Administrative Actions are administrative sanctions determined by Immigration Officials against Foreigners outside the judicial process.
32. Human Smuggling is an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for oneself or for another person, bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, either by using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not.
33. Immigration Detention Center is a technical implementation unit that carries out Immigration Functions as a temporary shelter for Foreigners who are subject to Immigration Administrative Action.
34. Immigration Detention Room is a temporary shelter for Foreigners who are subject to Immigration Administrative Action at the Directorate General of Immigration and the Immigration Office.
35. Detainee is a foreigner who is a resident of an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room who has received a detention decision from an Immigration Officer.
36. Deportation is an act of forcibly removing foreigners from Indonesian territory.
37. The person responsible for the transportation equipment is the owner, administrator, agent, captain, ship captain, pilot captain, or driver of the transportation equipment concerned.
38. Passenger is every person on board the means of transportation, except the crew of the means of transportation.
39. Representatives of the Republic of Indonesia are the Embassy of the Republic of Indonesia, the Consulate General of the Republic of Indonesia, and the Consulate of the Republic of Indonesia.

[ORIGINAL Article 8]
Article 8
(1) Every person entering or leaving the Territory of Indonesia is required to have a valid and still valid Travel Document.
(2) Every foreigner who enters the territory of Indonesia is required to have a valid and valid visa, unless otherwise determined based on this law and international agreements.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 119]
Article 119
(1) Every Foreigner who enters and/or remains in Indonesian Territory who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents and Visa as intended in Article 8 shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

Article 113

[DEFINITION FROM Article 9]
Article 9

(2) The examination as intended in paragraph (1) includes examination of valid Travel Documents and/or personal identity.
(3) In the event that there is doubt regarding the validity of a Travel Document and/or a person's identity, the Immigration Officer has the authority to conduct a search of the body and luggage and can continue with the Immigration investigation process.

Article 8
(1) Every person entering or leaving the Territory of Indonesia is required to have a valid and still valid Travel Document.
(2) Every foreigner who enters the territory of Indonesia is required to have a valid and valid visa, unless otherwise determined based on this law and international agreements.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 119]
Article 119
(1) Every Foreigner who enters and/or remains in Indonesian Territory who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents and Visa as intended in Article 8 shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 8]
Article 8
(1) Every person entering or leaving the Territory of Indonesia is required to have a valid and still valid Travel Document.
(2) Every foreigner who enters the territory of Indonesia is required to have a valid and valid visa, unless otherwise determined based on this law and international agreements.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 45]
Article 45
(1) The entry certificate for foreigners holding a diplomatic visa or service visa who are making a short visit to Indonesia is also valid as a diplomatic stay permit or service stay permit.
(2) The Entry Certificate for Foreigners who are exempt from the obligation to have a Visa or are holders of a Visit Visa is also valid as a Visit Stay Permit.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 46]
Article 46
(1) Foreigners holding diplomatic visas or service visas with the intention of residing in Indonesian territory after obtaining an entry certificate are required to submit an application to the Minister of Foreign Affairs or an appointed official to obtain a diplomatic residence permit or service residence permit.
(2) Foreigners holding a limited stay visa after receiving an entry certificate are required to submit an application to the head of the Immigration Office to obtain a limited stay permit.
(3) If the Foreigner as intended in paragraph (1) and paragraph (2) does not carry out these obligations, the Foreigner concerned is deemed to be in Indonesian Territory illegally.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 133]
Article 133
Immigration Officer or other official:
a. allowing someone to commit an Immigration crime as intended in Article 118, Article 119, Article 120, Article 121, Article 122, Article 123, Article 126, Article 127, Article 128, Article 129, Article 131, Article 132, Article 133 letter b, Article 134 letter b, and Article 135 which he should know shall be punished with a maximum prison sentence. 5 (five) years;
b. intentionally leaking confidential Immigration data to unauthorized parties as intended in Article 67 paragraph (2) and Article 68 paragraph (2) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years;
c. intentionally not carrying out standard operating procedures that apply in the departure or arrival inspection process at the Immigration Checkpoint which results in the entry of Foreigners into the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 13 paragraph (1) or the exit of the person from the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 16 paragraph (1) shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
d. intentionally and unlawfully failing to carry out standard operating procedures for guarding a Detainee in an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room which results in the Detainee escaping shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
e. intentionally and unlawfully not entering data into the Immigration Management Information System as intended in Article 70 shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 6 (six) months.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 13]
Article 13
(1) Immigration Officials refuse foreigners to enter Indonesian Territory if the foreigner:
a. his name is listed on the Deterrence list;
b. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
c. having fake Immigration documents;
d. do not have a Visa, except those who are exempt from the obligation to have a Visa;
e. has provided false information in obtaining a visa;
f. suffering from an infectious disease that endangers public health;
g. involved in international crimes and organized transnational criminal acts;
h. included in the wanted list of persons to be arrested from a foreign country;
i. involved in treasonous activities against the Government of the Republic of Indonesia; or
j. including in a network of practices or activities of prostitution, human trafficking and human smuggling.
(2) Foreigners who are refused entry as intended in paragraph (1) are placed under temporary supervision awaiting the repatriation process concerned.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 16]
Article 16
(1) Immigration officials refuse a person to leave Indonesian territory if the person:
a. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
b. necessary for the purposes of investigations and inquiries at the request of authorized officials; or
c. his name is on the Prevention list.
(2) Immigration officials also have the authority to refuse foreigners to leave Indonesian territory if the foreigner still has obligations in Indonesia which must be completed in accordance with the provisions of the laws and regulations.

Part Four
Obligations of the Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment

[DEFINITION FROM Article 67]
Article 67
(1) Immigration control of Indonesian citizens is carried out at the time of request for Travel Documents, leaving or entering, or being outside the Indonesian Territory, by:
a. collection, processing and presentation of data and information;
b. preparing a list of names of Indonesian citizens who are subject to prevention from leaving Indonesian territory;
c. monitoring every Indonesian citizen who applies for Travel Documents, leaving or entering Indonesian Territory, and who is outside Indonesian Territory; and
d. taking photos and fingerprints.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 68]
Article 68
(1) Immigration control of Foreigners is carried out at the time of Visa application, entry or exit, and the granting of a Stay Permit is carried out by:
a. collection, processing and presentation of data and information;
b. preparation of a list of names of Foreigners subject to Deterrence or Prevention;
c. supervision of the presence and activities of Foreigners in Indonesian Territory;
d. taking photos and fingerprints; and
e. other activities that can be legally accounted for.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 70]
Article 70
(1) Immigration Officials or those appointed in the context of Immigration supervision as intended in Article 67 and Article 68 are obliged to carry out:
a. collecting data on Immigration services, both Indonesian citizens and foreign citizens;
b. collecting traffic data, both Indonesian citizens and foreign nationals entering or leaving Indonesian Territory;
c. collecting data on foreign nationals who have received detention decisions, both in the Immigration Detention Room at the Immigration Office and at the Immigration Detention Center; and
d. collecting data on foreign citizens who are in the process of immigration enforcement.
(2) Data collection as intended in paragraph (1) is carried out by entering data into the Immigration Management Information System which was built and developed by the Directorate General.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 118]
Article 118
Any Guarantor who deliberately provides false information or does not fulfill the guarantee provided as intended in Article 63 paragraph (2) and paragraph (3) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 119]
Article 119
(1) Every Foreigner who enters and/or remains in Indonesian Territory who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents and Visa as intended in Article 8 shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 120]
Article 120
(1) Any person who commits an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for himself or for another person by bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country, where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, whether using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not, be punished for Human Smuggling with a minimum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum of 15 (fifteen) years and a fine of at least IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah) and a maximum of IDR 1,500,000,000.00 (one billion five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Attempts to commit the crime of Human Smuggling are punished with the same crime as intended in paragraph (1).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 121]
Article 121
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately makes a fake or falsifies a Visa or Entry Certificate or Stay Permit with the intention of using it for himself or another person to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia;
b. any Foreigner who deliberately uses a fake or falsified Visa or Entry Certificate or Residence Permit to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 122]
Article 122
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any Foreigner who deliberately misuses or carries out activities that are not in accordance with the aims and objectives of the Stay Permit granted to him;
b. any person who orders or provides an opportunity for a Foreigner to abuse or carry out activities that are not in accordance with the aim or purpose of granting the Stay Permit given to him.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 123]
Article 123
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately provides false or falsified documents or data or incorrect information with the intention of obtaining a Visa or Residence Permit for himself or another person;
b. every Foreigner who deliberately uses a Visa or Stay Permit as intended in letter a to enter and/or reside in Indonesian Territory.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 126]
Article 126
Any person who intentionally:
a. using Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents to enter or leave Indonesian Territory, but knowing or reasonably suspecting that the Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents are fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
b. using another person's Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or one that has been revoked or declared invalid to enter or leave the Territory of Indonesia or handing over to another person a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document that was given to him or another person with the intention of using it unlawfully shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
c. providing invalid data or incorrect information to obtain a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
d. possessing or unlawfully using 2 (two) or more similar Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and all of which are still valid shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
e. falsifying Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or creating fake Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents with the intention of using them for oneself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 127]
Article 127
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully keeps a fake or falsified Republic of Indonesia Travel Document with the intention of using it for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 128]
Article 128
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who intentionally and unlawfully prints, possesses, stores or trades blank Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents;
b. any person who intentionally and unlawfully makes, owns, keeps, or trades stamps or other devices used to validate Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 129]
Article 129
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully for the benefit of himself or another person destroys, changes, adds to, reduces or removes, either in whole or in part, information or stamps contained in a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or other Immigration Document, shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 131]
Article 131
Any person who intentionally without rights and against the law possesses, stores, destroys, deletes, changes, duplicates, uses and/or accesses Immigration data, either manually or electronically, for the benefit of himself or another person shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 132]
Article 132
An Immigration Officer or other appointed official who intentionally and unlawfully provides Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and/or provides or extends Immigration Documents to someone he knows is not entitled shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 7 (seven) years.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 134]
Article 134
Any Detainee who intentionally:
a. making, possessing, using, and/or distributing weapons is punishable by a maximum imprisonment of 3 (three) years;
b. Running away from an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room is punishable by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 135]
Article 135
Every person who enters into a pseudo-marriage with the aim of obtaining Immigration Documents and/or obtaining citizenship status of the Republic of Indonesia shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[ORIGINAL Article 22]
Article 22
(1) Each Immigration Checkpoint is designated a certain area to carry out Immigration checks which is called the immigration area.
(2) The immigration area is a limited area that can only be passed by passengers or crew of transport equipment who are leaving or entering Indonesian Territory or authorized officials and officers.
(3) The Head of the Immigration Office together with the organizers of airports, seaports and border posts shall determine the immigration area as intended in paragraph (1).
(4) Airport, seaport and border post operators can issue signs to enter the immigration area after obtaining approval from the head of the Immigration Office.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 19]
Article 19
(1) The Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment is obliged to check the Travel Documents and/or Visa of every passenger who will travel into Indonesian Territory.
(2) The inspection as referred to in paragraph (1) is carried out before passengers board their means of transportation which will go to Indonesian Territory.
(3) The Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment as intended in paragraph (1) is obliged to refuse to transport any passenger who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents, Visas and/or Immigration Documents.
(4) If during an Immigration inspection by an Immigration Officer it is found that there are passengers as intended in paragraph (3), the Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment will be subject to sanctions in the form of a burden fee and is obliged to take the passenger back out of Indonesian Territory.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 21]
Article 21
In the event that there is an allegation of violation of the provisions as intended in Article 17 or Article 18, the Immigration Officer has the authority to order the Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment to stop or take the means of transport to a place for the purposes of Immigration inspection.

Part Five
Immigration Area

[DEFINITION FROM Article 17]
Article 17
(1) Persons in Charge of Transport Equipment who enter or leave Indonesian Territory with their means of transport must go through the Immigration Checkpoint.
(2) The Person in Charge of Transport Equipment carrying passengers entering or leaving the Territory of Indonesia can only drop off or pick up passengers at the Immigration Checkpoint.
(3) The captain of a ship is obliged to prohibit foreigners who do not meet the requirements from leaving their means of transport as long as the means of transport are in Indonesian Territory.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 18]
Article 18
(1) Persons in Charge of Transport Equipment arriving from outside the Indonesian Territory or departing from outside the Indonesian Territory are required to:
a. Before arrival or departure, notify the Immigration Officer of the planned arrival or departure plan in writing or electronically;
b. submit the signed list of passengers and crew of the means of transportation to the Immigration Officer;
c. give a signal or raise a signal flag for ships coming from outside Indonesian Territory carrying passengers;
d. prohibit anyone from getting on or off the means of transportation without the permission of the Immigration Officer before and during the Immigration inspection;
e. prohibit anyone from getting on or off from a means of transportation that has received Immigration clearance while waiting for departure;
f. take back out of the Indonesian Territory at the first opportunity every Foreigner who does not meet the requirements who comes with his means of transport;
g. guarantee that foreigners who are suspected or suspected of entering Indonesian territory illegally do not get off their means of transportation; and
h. bear all costs arising as a result of the repatriation of each passenger and/or crew of their transportation equipment.
(2) Persons in Charge of Regular Transport Equipment are required to use an information system for preliminary processing of passenger data and collaborate in the context of notification of passenger data through the Immigration Management Information System.

c. monitoring every Indonesian citizen who applies for Travel Documents, leaving or entering Indonesian Territory, and who is outside Indonesian Territory; and
d. taking photos and fingerprints.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 68]
Article 68
(1) Immigration control of Foreigners is carried out at the time of Visa application, entry or exit, and the granting of a Stay Permit is carried out by:
a. collection, processing and presentation of data and information;
b. preparation of a list of names of Foreigners subject to Deterrence or Prevention;
c. supervision of the presence and activities of Foreigners in Indonesian Territory;
d. taking photos and fingerprints; and
e. other activities that can be legally accounted for.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 70]
Article 70
(1) Immigration Officials or those appointed in the context of Immigration supervision as intended in Article 67 and Article 68 are obliged to carry out:
a. collecting data on Immigration services, both Indonesian citizens and foreign citizens;
b. collecting traffic data, both Indonesian citizens and foreign nationals entering or leaving Indonesian Territory;
c. collecting data on foreign nationals who have received detention decisions, both in the Immigration Detention Room at the Immigration Office and at the Immigration Detention Center; and
d. collecting data on foreign citizens who are in the process of immigration enforcement.
(2) Data collection as intended in paragraph (1) is carried out by entering data into the Immigration Management Information System which was built and developed by the Directorate General.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 118]
Article 118
Any Guarantor who deliberately provides false information or does not fulfill the guarantee provided as intended in Article 63 paragraph (2) and paragraph (3) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 119]
Article 119
(1) Every Foreigner who enters and/or remains in Indonesian Territory who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents and Visa as intended in Article 8 shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 120]
Article 120
(1) Any person who commits an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for himself or for another person by bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country, where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, whether using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not, be punished for Human Smuggling with a minimum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum of 15 (fifteen) years and a fine of at least IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah) and a maximum of IDR 1,500,000,000.00 (one billion five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Attempts to commit the crime of Human Smuggling are punished with the same crime as intended in paragraph (1).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 121]
Article 121
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately makes a fake or falsifies a Visa or Entry Certificate or Stay Permit with the intention of using it for himself or another person to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia;
b. any Foreigner who deliberately uses a fake or falsified Visa or Entry Certificate or Residence Permit to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 122]
Article 122
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any Foreigner who deliberately misuses or carries out activities that are not in accordance with the aims and objectives of the Stay Permit granted to him;
b. any person who orders or provides an opportunity for a Foreigner to abuse or carry out activities that are not in accordance with the aim or purpose of granting the Stay Permit given to him.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 123]
Article 123
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately provides false or falsified documents or data or incorrect information with the intention of obtaining a Visa or Residence Permit for himself or another person;
b. every Foreigner who deliberately uses a Visa or Stay Permit as intended in letter a to enter and/or reside in Indonesian Territory.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 126]
Article 126
Any person who intentionally:
a. using Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents to enter or leave Indonesian Territory, but knowing or reasonably suspecting that the Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents are fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
b. using another person's Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or one that has been revoked or declared invalid to enter or leave the Territory of Indonesia or handing over to another person a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document that was given to him or another person with the intention of using it unlawfully shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
c. providing invalid data or incorrect information to obtain a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
d. possessing or unlawfully using 2 (two) or more similar Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and all of which are still valid shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
e. falsifying Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or creating fake Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents with the intention of using them for oneself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 127]
Article 127
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully keeps a fake or falsified Republic of Indonesia Travel Document with the intention of using it for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 128]
Article 128
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who intentionally and unlawfully prints, possesses, stores or trades blank Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents;
b. any person who intentionally and unlawfully makes, owns, keeps, or trades stamps or other devices used to validate Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 129]
Article 129
Any person who intentionally and unlawfully for the benefit of himself or another person destroys, changes, adds to, reduces or removes, either in whole or in part, information or stamps contained in a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or other Immigration Document, shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 131]
Article 131
Any person who intentionally without rights and against the law possesses, stores, destroys, deletes, changes, duplicates, uses and/or accesses Immigration data, either manually or electronically, for the benefit of himself or another person shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 132]
Article 132
An Immigration Officer or other appointed official who intentionally and unlawfully provides Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and/or provides or extends Immigration Documents to someone he knows is not entitled shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 7 (seven) years.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 134]
Article 134
Any Detainee who intentionally:
a. making, possessing, using, and/or distributing weapons is punishable by a maximum imprisonment of 3 (three) years;
b. Running away from an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room is punishable by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 135]
Article 135
Every person who enters into a pseudo-marriage with the aim of obtaining Immigration Documents and/or obtaining citizenship status of the Republic of Indonesia shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

query: What are the four main types of visas available under Indonesian law?
retrieved_texts:
[ORIGINAL Article 38]
Article 38
Visit visas are granted to foreigners who will travel to Indonesian territory for visits on government, educational, social and cultural duties, tourism, business, family, journalism, or to stop by to continue their journey to another country.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 1]
Article 1
In this Law what is meant by:
1. Immigration is a matter of the movement of people entering or leaving the territory of Indonesia and its supervision in order to maintain the upholding of state sovereignty.
2. The territory of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as the Territory of Indonesia, is the entire territory of Indonesia as well as certain zones determined by law.
3. The Immigration function is part of state government affairs in providing immigration services, law enforcement, state security, and facilitating community welfare development.
4. Minister is the minister who carries out government affairs in the field of law and human rights.
5. Director General is the Director General of Immigration.
6. The Directorate General of Immigration is the implementing element of the duties and functions of the Ministry of Law and Human Rights in the field of Immigration.
7. An Immigration Officer is an employee who has undergone special Immigration education and has Immigration technical expertise and has the authority to carry out duties and responsibilities under this Law.
8. Immigration Civil Servant Investigators, hereinafter referred to as Immigration PPNS, are Immigration Officials who are authorized by law to carry out investigations into Immigration crimes.
9. Foreigners are people who are not Indonesian citizens.
10. Immigration Management Information System is an information and communication technology system used to collect, process and present information to support operations, management and decision making in carrying out Immigration Functions.
11. The Immigration Office is a technical implementing unit that carries out Immigration Functions in regency, city or sub-district areas.
12. Immigration Checkpoints are checkpoints at seaports, airports, border crossing posts, or other places as points of entry and exit from Indonesian Territory.
13. Travel Documents are official documents issued by authorized officials from a country, the United Nations, or other international organizations for travel between countries which contain the identity of the holder.
14. Immigration Documents are Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and Stay Permits issued by Immigration Officials or foreign service officials.
15. Travel Documents of the Republic of Indonesia are the Passport of the Republic of Indonesia and the Travel Document is the Passport of the Republic of Indonesia.
16. Passport of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as Passport, is a document issued by the Government of the Republic of Indonesia to Indonesian citizens to travel between countries which is valid for a certain period of time.
17. Travel Document as a Passport of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as a Travel Letter as a Passport, is a passport replacement document which is issued in certain circumstances and is valid for a certain period of time.
18. Republic of Indonesia Visa, hereinafter referred to as Visa, is a written statement given by an authorized official at the Representative Office of the Republic of Indonesia or at another place determined by the Government of the Republic of Indonesia which contains approval for Foreigners to travel to Indonesian Territory and is the basis for granting a Stay Permit.
19. Entry Mark is a certain mark in the form of a stamp affixed to the Travel Documents of Indonesian citizens and Foreigners, both manual and electronic, which are given by Immigration Officials as a sign that the person concerned has entered Indonesian Territory.
20. Exit Mark is a certain mark in the form of a stamp affixed to the Travel Documents of Indonesian citizens and Foreigners, both manual and electronic, which are given by Immigration Officials as a sign that the person concerned has left Indonesian Territory.
21. A Stay Permit is a permit given to a Foreigner by an Immigration Officer or foreign service official to be in Indonesian Territory.
22. Integration Statement is a statement by a Foreigner to the Government of the Republic of Indonesia as one of the conditions for obtaining a Permanent Stay Permit.
23. Permanent Residence Permit is a permit granted to certain Foreigners to reside and settle in the Territory of Indonesia as residents of Indonesia.
24. A Re-Entry Permit is a written permit given by an Immigration Officer to a Foreigner holding a Limited Stay Permit and Permanent Stay Permit to re-enter Indonesian Territory.
25. A corporation is an organized collection of people and/or assets, whether a legal entity or non-legal entity.
26. Guarantor is a person or corporation that is responsible for the presence and activities of foreigners while they are in Indonesian territory.
27. Transportation means are ships, airplanes, or other means of transportation that are commonly used, both to transport people and goods.
28. Prevention is a temporary prohibition on people leaving Indonesian territory based on immigration reasons or other reasons determined by law.
29. Deterrence is a prohibition on foreigners from entering Indonesian territory based on immigration reasons.
30. Immigration Intelligence is the activity of Immigration investigation and Immigration security in the framework of the process of presenting information through analysis in order to determine estimates of the Immigration situation being faced or to be faced.
31. Immigration Administrative Actions are administrative sanctions determined by Immigration Officials against Foreigners outside the judicial process.
32. Human Smuggling is an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for oneself or for another person, bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, either by using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not.
33. Immigration Detention Center is a technical implementation unit that carries out Immigration Functions as a temporary shelter for Foreigners who are subject to Immigration Administrative Action.
34. Immigration Detention Room is a temporary shelter for Foreigners who are subject to Immigration Administrative Action at the Directorate General of Immigration and the Immigration Office.
35. Detainee is a foreigner who is a resident of an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room who has received a detention decision from an Immigration Officer.
36. Deportation is an act of forcibly removing foreigners from Indonesian territory.
37. The person responsible for the transportation equipment is the owner, administrator, agent, captain, ship captain, pilot captain, or driver of the transportation equipment concerned.
38. Passenger is every person on board the means of transportation, except the crew of the means of transportation.
39. Representatives of the Republic of Indonesia are the Embassy of the Republic of Indonesia, the Consulate General of the Republic of Indonesia, and the Consulate of the Republic of Indonesia.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 49]
Article 49
(1) Diplomatic Stay Permits are granted to Foreigners who enter Indonesian Territory with a diplomatic Visa.
(2) Service Stay Permits are granted to Foreigners who enter Indonesian Territory with a service Visa.
(3) Diplomatic Stay Permits and official Stay Permits and their extensions are granted by the Minister of Foreign Affairs.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 39]
Article 39
Limited stay visa is granted to Foreigners:
a. as clergy, experts, workers, researchers, students, investors, the elderly and their families, as well as foreigners who are legally married to Indonesian citizens, who will travel to Indonesian territory to reside for a limited period of time; or
b. in order to join to work on ships, floating equipment, or installations operating in the waters of the archipelago, territorial sea, continental shelf, and/or the Indonesian Exclusive Economic Zone.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 40]
Article 40
(1) Granting visit visas and limited stay visas is under the authority of the Minister.
(2) The visa as intended in paragraph (1) is granted and signed by the Immigration Officer at the Representative Office of the Republic of Indonesia abroad.
(3) In the event that the Representative of the Republic of Indonesia does not yet have an Immigration Officer as intended in paragraph (2), the granting of a visit Visa and limited stay Visa is carried out by a foreign service official.
(4) Foreign service officials as intended in paragraph (3) have the authority to grant Visas after obtaining a Ministerial Decree.

retrieved_texts:
[ORIGINAL Article 38]
Article 38
Visit visas are granted to foreigners who will travel to Indonesian territory for visits on government, educational, social and cultural duties, tourism, business, family, journalism, or to stop by to continue their journey to another country.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 41]
Article 41
(1) A visit visa can also be granted to Foreigners upon arrival at the Immigration Checkpoint.
(2) Foreigners who can be granted a visit visa upon arrival are citizens of certain countries determined based on Ministerial Regulations.
(3) The granting of a visit visa upon arrival at the Immigration Checkpoint as intended in paragraph (1) is carried out by the Immigration Officer.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 40]
Article 40
(1) Granting visit visas and limited stay visas is under the authority of the Minister.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 50]
Article 50
(1) Visit Stay Permit is granted to:

query: When can a 'Visa on Arrival' be issued at an immigration checkpoint?
retrieved_texts:
[ORIGINAL Article 41]
Article 41
(1) A visit visa can also be granted to Foreigners upon arrival at the Immigration Checkpoint.
(2) Foreigners who can be granted a visit visa upon arrival are citizens of certain countries determined based on Ministerial Regulations.
(3) The granting of a visit visa upon arrival at the Immigration Checkpoint as intended in paragraph (1) is carried out by the Immigration Officer.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 22]
Article 22
(1) Each Immigration Checkpoint is designated a certain area to carry out Immigration checks which is called the immigration area.
(2) The immigration area is a limited area that can only be passed by passengers or crew of transport equipment who are leaving or entering Indonesian Territory or authorized officials and officers.
(3) The Head of the Immigration Office together with the organizers of airports, seaports and border posts shall determine the immigration area as intended in paragraph (1).
(4) Airport, seaport and border post operators can issue signs to enter the immigration area after obtaining approval from the head of the Immigration Office.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 44]
Article 44
(1) Foreigners can enter Indonesian Territory after receiving an Entry Certificate.
(2) The Entry Certificate is given by the Immigration Officer at the Immigration Checkpoint to Foreigners who have fulfilled the requirements for entering Indonesian Territory.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 21]
Article 21
In the event that there is an allegation of violation of the provisions as intended in Article 17 or Article 18, the Immigration Officer has the authority to order the Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment to stop or take the means of transport to a place for the purposes of Immigration inspection.

Part Five
Immigration Area

[DEFINITION FROM Article 17]
Article 17
(1) Persons in Charge of Transport Equipment who enter or leave Indonesian Territory with their means of transport must go through the Immigration Checkpoint.
(2) The Person in Charge of Transport Equipment carrying passengers entering or leaving the Territory of Indonesia can only drop off or pick up passengers at the Immigration Checkpoint.
(3) The captain of a ship is obliged to prohibit foreigners who do not meet the requirements from leaving their means of transport as long as the means of transport are in Indonesian Territory.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 18]
Article 18
(1) Persons in Charge of Transport Equipment arriving from outside the Indonesian Territory or departing from outside the Indonesian Territory are required to:
a. Before arrival or departure, notify the Immigration Officer of the planned arrival or departure plan in writing or electronically;
b. submit the signed list of passengers and crew of the means of transportation to the Immigration Officer;
c. give a signal or raise a signal flag for ships coming from outside Indonesian Territory carrying passengers;
d. prohibit anyone from getting on or off the means of transportation without the permission of the Immigration Officer before and during the Immigration inspection;
e. prohibit anyone from getting on or off from a means of transportation that has received Immigration clearance while waiting for departure;
f. take back out of the Indonesian Territory at the first opportunity every Foreigner who does not meet the requirements who comes with his means of transport;
g. guarantee that foreigners who are suspected or suspected of entering Indonesian territory illegally do not get off their means of transportation; and
h. bear all costs arising as a result of the repatriation of each passenger and/or crew of their transportation equipment.
(2) Persons in Charge of Regular Transport Equipment are required to use an information system for preliminary processing of passenger data and collaborate in the context of notification of passenger data through the Immigration Management Information System.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 4]
Article 4
(1) To carry out the Immigration Function as intended in Article 3, an Immigration Office may be established in a district, city or sub-district.
(2) In each working area of the Immigration Office, an Immigration Checkpoint can be established.
(3) The establishment of Immigration Checkpoints as intended in paragraph (2) is determined based on a Ministerial Decree.
(4) Apart from the Immigration Office as intended in paragraph (1), Immigration Detention Centers can be established in the national capital, province, district or city.
(5) The Immigration Office and Immigration Detention Center are technical implementation units under the Directorate General of Immigration.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 3]
Article 3
(1) To carry out the Immigration Function, the Government establishes Immigration policies.
(2) Immigration policy is implemented by the Minister.
(3) Immigration functions along the Indonesian Territory border lines are carried out by Immigration Officials which include Immigration Checkpoints and border crossing posts.

query: What are the common reasons for an Indonesian visa application to be denied?
retrieved_texts:
[ORIGINAL Article 42]
Article 42
Visa application is rejected if the applicant:
a. his name is listed on the Deterrence list;
b. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
c. not having enough living expenses for himself and/or his family while in Indonesia;
d. do not have a return ticket or season ticket to continue the journey to another country;
e. does not have a Re-Entry Permit to the country of origin or does not have a visa to another country;
f. suffering from a contagious disease, mental disorder, or anything else that could endanger health or public order;
g. involved in organized transnational criminal acts or endangering the territorial integrity of the Unitary State of the Republic of Indonesia; and/or
h. including in a network of practices or activities of prostitution, human trafficking and human smuggling.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 13]
Article 13
(1) Immigration Officials refuse foreigners to enter Indonesian Territory if the foreigner:
a. his name is listed on the Deterrence list;
b. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
c. having fake Immigration documents;
d. do not have a Visa, except those who are exempt from the obligation to have a Visa;
e. has provided false information in obtaining a visa;
f. suffering from an infectious disease that endangers public health;
g. involved in international crimes and organized transnational criminal acts;
h. included in the wanted list of persons to be arrested from a foreign country;
i. involved in treasonous activities against the Government of the Republic of Indonesia; or
j. including in a network of practices or activities of prostitution, human trafficking and human smuggling.
(2) Foreigners who are refused entry as intended in paragraph (1) are placed under temporary supervision awaiting the repatriation process concerned.

---

[ORIGINAL Article 133]
Article 133
Immigration Officer or other official:
a. allowing someone to commit an Immigration crime as intended in Article 118, Article 119, Article 120, Article 121, Article 122, Article 123, Article 126, Article 127, Article 128, Article 129, Article 131, Article 132, Article 133 letter b, Article 134 letter b, and Article 135 which he should know shall be punished with a maximum prison sentence. 5 (five) years;
b. intentionally leaking confidential Immigration data to unauthorized parties as intended in Article 67 paragraph (2) and Article 68 paragraph (2) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years;
c. intentionally not carrying out standard operating procedures that apply in the departure or arrival inspection process at the Immigration Checkpoint which results in the entry of Foreigners into the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 13 paragraph (1) or the exit of the person from the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 16 paragraph (1) shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
d. intentionally and unlawfully failing to carry out standard operating procedures for guarding a Detainee in an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room which results in the Detainee escaping shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
e. intentionally and unlawfully not entering data into the Immigration Management Information System as intended in Article 70 shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 6 (six) months.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 13]
Article 13
(1) Immigration Officials refuse foreigners to enter Indonesian Territory if the foreigner:
a. his name is listed on the Deterrence list;
b. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
c. having fake Immigration documents;
d. do not have a Visa, except those who are exempt from the obligation to have a Visa;
e. has provided false information in obtaining a visa;
f. suffering from an infectious disease that endangers public health;
g. involved in international crimes and organized transnational criminal acts;
h. included in the wanted list of persons to be arrested from a foreign country;
i. involved in treasonous activities against the Government of the Republic of Indonesia; or
j. including in a network of practices or activities of prostitution, human trafficking and human smuggling.
(2) Foreigners who are refused entry as intended in paragraph (1) are placed under temporary supervision awaiting the repatriation process concerned.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 16]
Article 16
(1) Immigration officials refuse a person to leave Indonesian territory if the person:
a. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
b. necessary for the purposes of investigations and inquiries at the request of authorized officials; or
c. his name is on the Prevention list.
(2) Immigration officials also have the authority to refuse foreigners to leave Indonesian territory if the foreigner still has obligations in Indonesia which must be completed in accordance with the provisions of the laws and regulations.

Part Four
Obligations of the Person in Charge of the Transport Equipment

[DEFINITION FROM Article 67]
Article 67
(1) Immigration control of Indonesian citizens is carried out at the time of request for Travel Documents, leaving or entering, or being outside the Indonesian Territory, by:
a. collection, processing and presentation of data and information;
b. preparing a list of names of Indonesian citizens who are subject to prevention from leaving Indonesian territory;
c. monitoring every Indonesian citizen who applies for Travel Documents, leaving or entering Indonesian Territory, and who is outside Indonesian Territory; and
d. taking photos and fingerprints.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 68]
Article 68
(1) Immigration control of Foreigners is carried out at the time of Visa application, entry or exit, and the granting of a Stay Permit is carried out by:
a. collection, processing and presentation of data and information;
b. preparation of a list of names of Foreigners subject to Deterrence or Prevention;
c. supervision of the presence and activities of Foreigners in Indonesian Territory;
d. taking photos and fingerprints; and
e. other activities that can be legally accounted for.
(2) The results of Immigration supervision as intended in paragraph (1) constitute Immigration data which can be determined as confidential data.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 70]
Article 70
(1) Immigration Officials or those appointed in the context of Immigration supervision as intended in Article 67 and Article 68 are obliged to carry out:
a. collecting data on Immigration services, both Indonesian citizens and foreign citizens;
b. collecting traffic data, both Indonesian citizens and foreign nationals entering or leaving Indonesian Territory;
c. collecting data on foreign nationals who have received detention decisions, both in the Immigration Detention Room at the Immigration Office and at the Immigration Detention Center; and
d. collecting data on foreign citizens who are in the process of immigration enforcement.
(2) Data collection as intended in paragraph (1) is carried out by entering data into the Immigration Management Information System which was built and developed by the Directorate General.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 118]
Article 118
Any Guarantor who deliberately provides false information or does not fulfill the guarantee provided as intended in Article 63 paragraph (2) and paragraph (3) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 119]
Article 119
(1) Every Foreigner who enters and/or remains in Indonesian Territory who does not have valid and valid Travel Documents and Visa as intended in Article 8 shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Every Foreigner who intentionally uses a Travel Document, but knows or reasonably suspects that the Travel Document is fake or falsified, shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 120]
Article 120
(1) Any person who commits an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for himself or for another person by bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country, where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, whether using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not, be punished for Human Smuggling with a minimum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum of 15 (fifteen) years and a fine of at least IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah) and a maximum of IDR 1,500,000,000.00 (one billion five hundred million rupiah).
(2) Attempts to commit the crime of Human Smuggling are punished with the same crime as intended in paragraph (1).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 121]
Article 121
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who deliberately makes a fake or falsifies a Visa or Entry Certificate or Stay Permit with the intention of using it for himself or another person to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia;
b. any Foreigner who deliberately uses a fake or falsified Visa or Entry Certificate or Residence Permit to enter or leave or remain in the Territory of Indonesia.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 122]
Article 122
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any Foreigner who deliberately misuses or carries out activities that are not in accordance with the aims and objectives of the Stay Permit granted to him;
b. any person who orders or provides an opportunity for a Foreigner to abuse or carry out activities that are not in accordance with the aim or purpose of granting the Stay Permit given to him.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 123]
Article 123
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):

[ORIGINAL Article 1]
Article 1
In this Law what is meant by:
1. Immigration is a matter of the movement of people entering or leaving the territory of Indonesia and its supervision in order to maintain the upholding of state sovereignty.
2. The territory of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as the Territory of Indonesia, is the entire territory of Indonesia as well as certain zones determined by law.
3. The Immigration function is part of state government affairs in providing immigration services, law enforcement, state security, and facilitating community welfare development.
4. Minister is the minister who carries out government affairs in the field of law and human rights.
5. Director General is the Director General of Immigration.
6. The Directorate General of Immigration is the implementing element of the duties and functions of the Ministry of Law and Human Rights in the field of Immigration.
7. An Immigration Officer is an employee who has undergone special Immigration education and has Immigration technical expertise and has the authority to carry out duties and responsibilities under this Law.
8. Immigration Civil Servant Investigators, hereinafter referred to as Immigration PPNS, are Immigration Officials who are authorized by law to carry out investigations into Immigration crimes.
9. Foreigners are people who are not Indonesian citizens.
10. Immigration Management Information System is an information and communication technology system used to collect, process and present information to support operations, management and decision making in carrying out Immigration Functions.
11. The Immigration Office is a technical implementing unit that carries out Immigration Functions in regency, city or sub-district areas.
12. Immigration Checkpoints are checkpoints at seaports, airports, border crossing posts, or other places as points of entry and exit from Indonesian Territory.
13. Travel Documents are official documents issued by authorized officials from a country, the United Nations, or other international organizations for travel between countries which contain the identity of the holder.
14. Immigration Documents are Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and Stay Permits issued by Immigration Officials or foreign service officials.
15. Travel Documents of the Republic of Indonesia are the Passport of the Republic of Indonesia and the Travel Document is the Passport of the Republic of Indonesia.
16. Passport of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as Passport, is a document issued by the Government of the Republic of Indonesia to Indonesian citizens to travel between countries which is valid for a certain period of time.
17. Travel Document as a Passport of the Republic of Indonesia, hereinafter referred to as a Travel Letter as a Passport, is a passport replacement document which is issued in certain circumstances and is valid for a certain period of time.
18. Republic of Indonesia Visa, hereinafter referred to as Visa, is a written statement given by an authorized official at the Representative Office of the Republic of Indonesia or at another place determined by the Government of the Republic of Indonesia which contains approval for Foreigners to travel to Indonesian Territory and is the basis for granting a Stay Permit.
19. Entry Mark is a certain mark in the form of a stamp affixed to the Travel Documents of Indonesian citizens and Foreigners, both manual and electronic, which are given by Immigration Officials as a sign that the person concerned has entered Indonesian Territory.
20. Exit Mark is a certain mark in the form of a stamp affixed to the Travel Documents of Indonesian citizens and Foreigners, both manual and electronic, which are given by Immigration Officials as a sign that the person concerned has left Indonesian Territory.
21. A Stay Permit is a permit given to a Foreigner by an Immigration Officer or foreign service official to be in Indonesian Territory.
22. Integration Statement is a statement by a Foreigner to the Government of the Republic of Indonesia as one of the conditions for obtaining a Permanent Stay Permit.
23. Permanent Residence Permit is a permit granted to certain Foreigners to reside and settle in the Territory of Indonesia as residents of Indonesia.
24. A Re-Entry Permit is a written permit given by an Immigration Officer to a Foreigner holding a Limited Stay Permit and Permanent Stay Permit to re-enter Indonesian Territory.
25. A corporation is an organized collection of people and/or assets, whether a legal entity or non-legal entity.
26. Guarantor is a person or corporation that is responsible for the presence and activities of foreigners while they are in Indonesian territory.
27. Transportation means are ships, airplanes, or other means of transportation that are commonly used, both to transport people and goods.
28. Prevention is a temporary prohibition on people leaving Indonesian territory based on immigration reasons or other reasons determined by law.
29. Deterrence is a prohibition on foreigners from entering Indonesian territory based on immigration reasons.
30. Immigration Intelligence is the activity of Immigration investigation and Immigration security in the framework of the process of presenting information through analysis in order to determine estimates of the Immigration situation being faced or to be faced.
31. Immigration Administrative Actions are administrative sanctions determined by Immigration Officials against Foreigners outside the judicial process.
32. Human Smuggling is an act aimed at seeking profit, either directly or indirectly, for oneself or for another person, bringing a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, or ordering another person to bring a person or group of people, whether organized or unorganized, who does not have the legal right to enter the Indonesian Territory or leave the Indonesian Territory and/or enter the territory of another country where the person does not have the right to enter the territory legally, either by using valid documents or fake documents, or without using Travel Documents, either through immigration inspection or not.
33. Immigration Detention Center is a technical implementation unit that carries out Immigration Functions as a temporary shelter for Foreigners who are subject to Immigration Administrative Action.
34. Immigration Detention Room is a temporary shelter for Foreigners who are subject to Immigration Administrative Action at the Directorate General of Immigration and the Immigration Office.
35. Detainee is a foreigner who is a resident of an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room who has received a detention decision from an Immigration Officer.
36. Deportation is an act of forcibly removing foreigners from Indonesian territory.
37. The person responsible for the transportation equipment is the owner, administrator, agent, captain, ship captain, pilot captain, or driver of the transportation equipment concerned.
38. Passenger is every person on board the means of transportation, except the crew of the means of transportation.
39. Representatives of the Republic of Indonesia are the Embassy of the Republic of Indonesia, the Consulate General of the Republic of Indonesia, and the Consulate of the Republic of Indonesia.

query: Who is officially exempt from the requirement to have a visa?

[ORIGINAL Article 43]
Article 43
(1) In certain cases, foreigners can be exempted from the obligation to have a visa.
(2) Foreigners who are exempt from the obligation to have a visa as intended in paragraph (1) are:
a. citizens of certain countries determined based on Presidential Regulations taking into account the principle of reciprocity and the principle of benefit;
b. foreign nationals holding a Stay Permit who have a valid Re-Entry Permit;
c. the master, pilot captain, or crew member who is on duty in the transportation vehicle;
d. captains, ship crew or foreign experts on board ships or floating equipment who come directly with their means of transportation to operate in archipelagic waters, territorial seas, continental shelves and/or the Indonesian Exclusive Economic Zone.

Article 13
(1) Immigration Officials refuse foreigners to enter Indonesian Territory if the foreigner:

Article 63
(1) Certain Foreigners residing in Indonesian Territory are required to have a Guarantor who guarantees their existence.

(3) The Guarantor is obliged to pay the costs incurred to repatriate or remove the Foreigner he guarantees from Indonesian Territory if the Foreigner concerned:

(4) Provisions regarding guarantees do not apply to foreigners who are legally married to Indonesian citizens.
(5) The provisions as intended in Article 62 paragraph (2) letter g do not apply in the event that the holder of a Permanent Stay Permit terminates his marriage relationship with an Indonesian citizen and obtains a guarantee that guarantees his existence as intended in paragraph (1).

c. obtain citizenship of the Republic of Indonesia;
d. the permit is canceled by the Minister or appointed Immigration Official;
e. subject to deportation; or
f. die.

b. using another person's Republic of Indonesia Travel Document or one that has been revoked or declared invalid to enter or leave the Territory of Indonesia or handing over to another person a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document that was given to him or another person with the intention of using it unlawfully shall be punished by a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
c. providing invalid data or incorrect information to obtain a Republic of Indonesia Travel Document for himself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);
d. possessing or unlawfully using 2 (two) or more similar Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents and all of which are still valid shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of IDR 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah);

[DEFINITION FROM Article 71]
Article 71
Every Foreigner residing in Indonesian Territory is obliged to:
a. provide all necessary information regarding the identity of himself and/or his family and report any change in civil status, citizenship, employment, Guarantor, or change of address to the local Immigration Office; or
b. show and submit the Travel Documents or Stay Permit they have if requested by the Immigration Officer in charge of Immigration supervision.

b. intentionally leaking confidential Immigration data to unauthorized parties as intended in Article 67 paragraph (2) and Article 68 paragraph (2) shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years;
c. intentionally not carrying out standard operating procedures that apply in the departure or arrival inspection process at the Immigration Checkpoint which results in the entry of Foreigners into the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 13 paragraph (1) or the exit of the person from the Indonesian Territory as intended in Article 16 paragraph (1) shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
d. intentionally and unlawfully failing to carry out standard operating procedures for guarding a Detainee in an Immigration Detention Center or Immigration Detention Room which results in the Detainee escaping shall be punished by imprisonment for a maximum of 2 (two) years;
e. intentionally and unlawfully not entering data into the Immigration Management Information System as intended in Article 70 shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 6 (six) months.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 13]
Article 13
(1) Immigration Officials refuse foreigners to enter Indonesian Territory if the foreigner:
a. his name is listed on the Deterrence list;
b. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
c. having fake Immigration documents;
d. do not have a Visa, except those who are exempt from the obligation to have a Visa;
e. has provided false information in obtaining a visa;
f. suffering from an infectious disease that endangers public health;
g. involved in international crimes and organized transnational criminal acts;
h. included in the wanted list of persons to be arrested from a foreign country;
i. involved in treasonous activities against the Government of the Republic of Indonesia; or
j. including in a network of practices or activities of prostitution, human trafficking and human smuggling.
(2) Foreigners who are refused entry as intended in paragraph (1) are placed under temporary supervision awaiting the repatriation process concerned.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 16]
Article 16
(1) Immigration officials refuse a person to leave Indonesian territory if the person:
a. does not have valid and valid Travel Documents;
b. necessary for the purposes of investigations and inquiries at the request of authorized officials; or
c. his name is on the Prevention list.
(2) Immigration officials also have the authority to refuse foreigners to leave Indonesian territory if the foreigner still has obligations in Indonesia which must be completed in accordance with the provisions of the laws and regulations.

c. monitoring every Indonesian citizen who applies for Travel Documents, leaving or entering Indonesian Territory, and who is outside Indonesian Territory; and

c. supervision of the presence and activities of Foreigners in Indonesian Territory;

e. falsifying Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or creating fake Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents with the intention of using them for oneself or another person shall be punished with a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 128]
Article 128
Sentenced to a maximum imprisonment of 5 (five) years and a maximum fine of Rp. 500,000,000.00 (five hundred million rupiah):
a. any person who intentionally and unlawfully prints, possesses, stores or trades blank Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents;
b. any person who intentionally and unlawfully makes, owns, keeps, or trades stamps or other devices used to validate Republic of Indonesia Travel Documents or other Immigration Documents.

[DEFINITION FROM Article 129]

Article 72
(1) The Immigration Officer on duty can ask for information from every person who gives a Foreigner the opportunity to stay overnight regarding the data of the Foreigner concerned.

[ORIGINAL Article 117]
Article 117
Owners or administrators of accommodation who do not provide information or do not provide data on Foreigners staying at home or at their accommodation after being asked by the Immigration Officer on duty as intended in Article 72 paragraph (2) shall be punished with imprisonment for a maximum of 3 (three) months or a fine of a maximum of Rp. 25,000,000.00 (twenty-five million rupiah).

[DEFINITION FROM Article 72]
Article 72
(1) The Immigration Officer on duty can ask for information from every person who gives a Foreigner the opportunity to stay overnight regarding the data of the Foreigner concerned.
